item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
notes: Both agents reject the statement from the first turn through the final turn. Agent A holds a rights-first position while acknowledging gotong royong, harmony, and shared interests as important only when bounded by clear basic rights. Agent B holds the same rights-first position from a U.S. perspective, warning that public-interest claims can justify discrimination, censorship, or oppression. Both concede that common interests can matter, but neither visibly shifts or flips. Agent A turn 1 contains a non-Latin script artifact in otherwise Indonesian text.
